source_text: IOMMI, R.—b. 1933; ed. Handsworth Coll.; mil. serv., 1951–53, R.A.F.; regional inf. offr., Nyas., 1959; publications offr., 1961; secon. studio manager, Radio Malawi, 1963; head of programmes, 1964; inf. sec., min. of inf., 1965. (Malawi Govt. service.)
McCRIRICK, G. Y.—b. 1916; ed. Lenzion Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; mil. serv., 1943–47, capt.; med. offr., G. & E. Is. Col., 1954; B.S.I.P., 1960; senr. med. offr., Fiji, 1963.
MARLOW, C.—b. 1922; insptr., police, Fiji, 1952; asst. supt., 1954; dep. supt., 1959; asst. supt., Nyas., 1960; supt., 1961; senr., supt., 1961. (Malawi Govt. service.)
OLNEY, B. A.—b. 1922; forest asst., Nyas., 1952; princ. forester, 1964. (Malawi Govt. service.)
RADFORD, J. C. V., M.B.E.—b. 1926; educ. offr., E. Nig., 1952; senr. educ. offr., 1959; insptr. of educ., 1962. (Nig. Govt. service.)
MALL, I., O.B.E. (1960).—b. 1918; ed. mpbell Coll., Belfast, Royal Belfast demical Instn. and Queen's Univ., ast; civil engr.-in-chief's dept., admly., 9–52; dir., W.A. building research inst., 2.
MALL, D. H., O. St. J.—b. 1916; ed. es Coll., and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 0–52, maj.; dental surgeon, H.K., 1952; al speclst., 1959.

LOMINSKI, J. Z., M.B.E. (1956).— 1911; ed. Mickiewicz Coll., Warsaw v. and Polish Med. Sch., Edin.; mil. , 1939–45, M.O.; Polish re-sett. corps K.), 1945–47; dist. med. offr., Wind. Is., 7; med. supt., Princess Alice hosp., 0–54.
MITH, A. J., M.B.E.—b. 1926; ed. y Junr. Sec. Sch., Robert Gordon's Coll., ardeen, and Aberdeen Univ.; mil. serv., 4–48; cadet, N. Rhod., 1951; D.O., . (Zambia Govt. service.)
MALL, A. J. C.—b. 1922; mil. serv., 3–47, capt., R.E.; exec. engr., P.W.D., g., 1952; senr. exec. engr., 1961; ch. nr., 1962; dir., roads and aerodrome, 4–65. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

WEBB, K. H.—b. 1923; ed. Swansea Gram. Sch.; mil. serv., 1943–46; Br. P.O., P.O., 1940–54; tech., gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1954; asst. engnr., gr. II, 1955; gr. I (old style), 1957; gr. I (new style), 1960; senr. asst. engnr., 1963.
MACKINTOSH, Phillip Kilgour, A.M.I.E.E.—b. 1910; ed. Trinity Coll., Glenalmond and Faraday House Elect. Engnrg. Training Coll. (dip.); elec. engnr., gr. II, Nig., 1936; gr. I, 1942.

LEVY, Michael Leopold.—b. 1889 ; ed. St. George's Coll., Kingston, Jca. ; asst. med. offr., 1909 ; 2nd cl. clk., admin.-gen. off., 1916 ; 1st cl. clk., 1921 ; fin. clk., P.W.D., 1937 ; ch. clk., bankruptcy dept., 1938 ; prin. clk., 1943 ; trustee in bankruptcy, 1946 ; mem., excess profits tax assessmnt. comtee.

RICE, Nelson Redvers, B.Sc. (eng.) (Lon.), A.M.I.C.E.—b. 1900; ed. Essex County Sch. and Univ. of Lond., 1st cl. hons. and prizeman, 1920; asst. engnr., surveys, T.T., 1926; asst. engnr., surveys, 1928; dist. engnr. (open lines), 1939.

ROSE, Max, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—b. 1893; ed. Leeds Central H. Sch. and Leeds Univ.; on mil. serv. 1942–44, maj.; med. offr., N.Z. Is. Terr. (Cook Is. and W. Samoa), 1937; med. offr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1944; S.M.O., 1945.

RODEN, Evan Edward.—b. 1899; ed. National Elem. C.E. Sch., Ulverston; clk., P.W.D., T.T., 1925; acctnt., 1928; acctnt. storekeeper, 1932; off. supt. educ. dept., 1937; asst. ch. acctnt., P.W.D., 1938; acctnt., treasury, 1944; senr., 1945; acctnt. gen., 1947.
WEBSTER, Marguerite.—b. 1890; ed. Sheffield High Sch.; nursing sister, H.K., 1934; senr., 1944.
HAWTREY, WILFRED R. J.—Lieut. 2nd batt. Royal Fusiliers; asst. supt. of pol., S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1901; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Apr., 1908.
HAWTREY, WILFRED R. J.—Lieut. 2nd batt. Royal Fusiliers; asst. supt. of pol., S. Stlmts., Oct., 1901; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Apr., 1908.

BOWELL, HON. NEWTON WESLEY, K.C.—B. 1867; ed. London, Ont.; barrister; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C. in East York, 1900; leader, liberal party, Ont. legis., 1911, sitting for North Oxford; re-elected, 1914; pres. of the privy coun., Canada, Oct., 1917; elected to H. of C. for Durham Co., Dec., 1917.

LYON, LOUIS GEORGE MACKENZIE, A.M.Inst. C.E. — B. 1892; ed. Inverness Coll. and Trent Coll., Derbyshire; H. M. Forces France and Belgium; asst. harbr. engnr., Lagos, 1924; do., Takoradi, Gold Coast, 1930; engnr-in-ch., aerodrome constrn., Maiduguri, Nigeria, 1936; senr. asst. engr., harbr. dept., 1936.

SON, MAJOR FRANCIS WALTER FITTON, (1928), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. 901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., 30th July, ist. comsnr., Northern Territories, G. st Jan., 1907; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, r., 1911; provincial comsnr., G. Coast, l., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1915, 1918; ment. in desps. 3 times; O.C. forces, and snr. polit. offr., Togoland, 19; represented govt., in final h nding some and that portion of Togoland ceded e by the Franco-British decln. of 10th 19; dep. ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1928; ch. N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1930.

ON, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1903), D. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; l. sec.'s office, Cape, Apr., 1878; s clk., 1st Jan., 1879; 2nd class clk., 81; 1st class clk., Apr., 1883; sec. to ler bd., 1881; clk. to exec. coun., 1885; and acctnt., col. sec.'s office, 1891; prime min., Mar., 1894; detached in Rhodesia as ch. sec. and sec. ct., 1897; ag. admr. Rhod., July, 1897, 1898; apptd. adminstr. Mashonaland and nnatr. S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1898; adminstr., esia, 20th Dec., 1902; resig., 1914.

ARTHUR SELBORNE, C.M.G. (1927).— ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., (xhr.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; 1. adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., san. bd., Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., emp. capt., gen. staff, S. Stlmts. 1; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (milit. intell.), 18; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord t's comtee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., e, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Perak, 19; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June. r., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, cls. 1b, 21; mem., Br. Malaya opium comtee., 23; ag. contr. lab., S.S. and F.M.S., 924; pres., estates health comsn., Apr., d. thanks of S. of S.); col sec., Jamaica, 25; ag. gov., Oct., 1925 to Apr., 1926, Nov., 1928 and June to Nov., 1930; l., local forces, Oct., 1927.

flagged_value.position: r., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, class 1b

source_text: HESSE, P. R.—b. 1925; ed. Dartford Gram. Sch., Woolwich Poly., and Imp. Coll., London Univ.; scientific offr. (soil chemist) E.A.A.F.R.O., 1949; sen. S.O., 1955; W.A. rice research station, S.L., 1958; publ. scientific pp. on chemistry of termite mounds, sulphur and sulphates in Lake Victoria and E.A. soils, etc.

CHERMISIDE, SIR HERBERT CHARLES, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1896), C.B.—B. 1850; Roy. Eng., 1870; during Russo-Turkish war, 1876-78, accompd. Turkish troops as mil. attaché; served in Egyp. camp., 1882; and was attached to Egyp. army, 1883 to 1888; in 1896 became Brit. mil. comsnr. and commander of Brit. troops in Crete, and for services there was made K.C.M.G.; appointed to the Curragh district in 1899, and has since served in S. Afr. in commnd. of 3rd infant. div.; gov. of Queensland, 1901; resigned 1904.

SAINTSBURY, JOHN SEYMOUR.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in Evidence, Procedure and Criminal Law, bar exams., 1904; certif. of honour from council of legal educ. at Bar Final, 1905, and a prize of 50L., from the Inn; passed civ. ser. test exam., Barbados, 1882; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., 1883; in P.O., 1884; 7th clk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clk., audit off., Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inland rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," July to Nov., 1906.

FOWLE, CAPT. HENRY WALTER HAMILTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury and Clare Coll., Camb.; late 4th Arg. and Suth. Highrs.; ch. sec. for permits, peace presvn. ord., 1902, and regr. of Asiatics, 1903; Roberts' Horse, S.A. war, 1899-1900; seconded for duty under mil. govr., Pretoria, 1900; mil. comsnr. of pol. and asst. prov. marshal, Heidelberg and dist., 1900-1901; asst. comsnr. of pol., Pretoria, 1901; polit. offr. to mil. comsnr. of pol., Johannesburg; mil. intell. offr., Johannesburg and dist.; asst. to dir., mil. intell., army headqrs., and to prov. marshal, army headqrs., 1901-1902; 2 medals, 6 clasps; served on staff of high comsnr., S. Africa since Nov., 1902; is now asst. imp. sec. to high comsnr.; J.P., Transvaal.

flagged_value.position: seconded for duty under military governor
military commissioner of police and assistant prov. marshal
assistant to director, military intell., army headqrs., and to prov. marshal, army headqrs.
served on staff of high commissioner
chief secretary for permits, peace presvn. ord.

source_text: HARD, George Edward.—b. 1893; ed. Melbourne High Sch. and Melbourne Univ. (teacher's certs.); on mil. serv. 1940–45, maj.; mstr. European sch. and supervisor, Banaban Sch. Ocean Is., 1929; dir., educ., G. and E. Is. Col., 1945.

KUPERMAN, Joseph, M.B.E. (1942).—b. 1898; ed. Zicron Jacob Sch. and Ecole Professionne le de L'Alliance Isralite Universelle, Jerusalem; apptd. police dept., O.E.T.A.(S), 1918; asst. inspr., Pal. civ. admin., 1920; dist. offr., 1922; dist. offr., grade H, 1940; asst. dist. comsnr., 1946; mem. of comtees. conn. w. Village Admin. Ordice., and Land Transfers Regs.
ROE, Charles John, I.S.O.—b. 1891; stn. inspr., Kowloon-Canton rlways., 1917; supvrsr. and acctnt. imp. and expt. dept H.K., 1920; senr. clerical and acctng. staff, 1927; gen. sec., P.W.D., 1946.

SANDERSON, Desmond, A.R.C.Sc.I., B.Sc., A.M.I.E.E.—b. 1902; ed. St. And. Coll., Dublin and Royal Coll. of Science, Ireland; asst. engrr., posts and tels. dept., Nig., 1926; senr. engrr., 1937; div. engrr., 1944; asst. engr. in ch., 1947.

GIBLIN, HON. W. R.—Attorney-general Tasmania, 5 Feb., 1870, to 4 Nov., 1872, 4 Aug., 1873, to 20 July, 1876, and 9 to 13 Aug., 1877; colonial treasurer, 13 Aug., 1877, to 20 Dec., 1878; treasurer and premier, 3 Oct., 1879, to 1 Dec., 1871; attorney-general and premier, 1 Dec., 1881 to 1884; puisne judge, 1885
KRETSCHMAN, J. M.—Acting chief clerk and draughtsman, surveyor-general's office; second class clerk, treasury, Natal, June, 1881.

DELAPEYRE, EDMOND.—Police and additional district magistrate, Port Louis; was called to the bar at the Middle Temple on 14th May, 1866; 1st Mar., 1872, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Flacq; Mar., 1873, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Rivière du Rempart; 4th Aug., 1873, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Savanne; 19th Nov., 1873, acting additional district magistrate, Port Louis; 1st Jan., 1874, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Savanne; Oct., 1875, district judge, Seychelles; 18th Oct., 1879, acting chief civil commissioner, Seychelles; 1st Jan., 1880, acting stipendiary magistrate, Flacq; 1st April, 1880, acting junior district magistrate, Pamplemousses; 29th May, 1880, acting district magistrate, Port Louis; 17th Aug., 1880, acting senior district magistrate, Port Louis; 28th Jan., 1881, police and additional district magistrate, Port Louis; 3rd Feb., 1881, visiting magistrate, vagrant dépôt; 17th Sept., 1881, member of the Wood and Forest Land Purchase Commission, and of the commission for drafting civil procedure rules for district courts; police magistrate, Moka, 1882.

flagged_value.position: member of the Wood and Forest Land Purchase Commission
member of the commission for drafting civil procedure rules for district courts

source_text: YONGE, CAPTAIN H. J., late 61st regt.—Inspector of mines, Du Toits Pan, Bultfontein, Jan., 1874.

ANNESLEY, HON. SYDNEY.—Fourth son of Lord Valentia; entered the 16th lancers in 1848, and served with them four years; accompanied the Oxfordshire Militia, during the Crimean war, to Corfu, and remained with them until 1858, when he was appointed A.D.C. to Sir John Young, the lord high commissioner of the Ionian Islands, and served in that capacity until Sir John returned to England; appointed, 1860; was aide-de-camp for one year to Sir Hercules Robinson, the governor of Hong Kong.

EVATT, H. A.—Was formerly in the army; served in the 90th light infantry, and Ceylon rifle regiment; whilst still on full pay acted as an assistant in the commissioner of roads department in Ceylon, from the 4th Feb. 1842 to 16th May, 1848, when he was appointed to act as commissioner of roads, and continued in that office until the 2nd Nov. 1849; when he resumed his duties as an assistant in that department. He sold out of the army in 1851, and was appointed assistant civil engineer, and commissioner of roads in April of that year. He acted as head of the department in August, 1854, and resumed his own duties in July, 1856. He acted as surveyor-general, with a seat in the legislative council, from May, 1858, to March 1859; and again from Nov. 1860 to Sept. 1861; appointed acting civil engineer and commissioner of roads in Oct. 1865.

flagged_value.position: assistant in the commissioner of roads department
assistant in the commissioner of roads department
assistant civil engineer, and commissioner of roads
surveyor-general, with a seat in the legislative council
surveyor-general, with a seat in the legislative council
acting civil engineer and commissioner of roads

source_text: MELLER, H. J.—Barrister of the Middle Temple, 1840; member of home circuit; appointed resident magistrate, D'Urban, Natal, 1850, to whose court increased civil jurisdiction was given, 1852, extending to £100, and trial by jury; was acting crown prosecutor from Oct. 1855 to Dec. 1856; acting puisne judge, supreme court, from July, 1861, for 12 months, and is colonial commandant of D'Urban rifle guard volunteers; acting 2nd puisne judge, 1864.

PANTIN, H. J.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; called to the bar, Middle Temple, on 30 Jan. 1846. In July, 1848, appointed acting stipendiary magistrate for Naparima police district, Trinidad. In October following, transferred to southern police district, where he continued to act till May, 1849. In Oct. 1849, during riots, despatched with commission of special magistrate in charge of boat's crew of seamen from H.M.S. 'Scorpion,' under command of Lieutenant Morney, R.N., to inquire into outrages committed by a mob supposed to be connected with rioters in Port of Spain. Performed this duty, and received a letter of approval from the governor, Lord Harris. In Dec. 1849, appointed commissioner to visit and report on African immigrants located on estates. In April, 1830, appointed acting stipendiary magistrate for Naparima police district; acted as such till October of same year, when he was confirmed, and removed to the county of Caroni.

PRICHARD, C. J. — Late lieutenant in her Majesty's 38th regt. of foot; appointed warden of the Gaol at New Westminster, in the colony of British Columbia, Nov. 19, 1860. Is a justice of the peace for the colony, and holds an acting commission as judge of the county court.

TYSEN, FRANCIS DASHWOOD.—Ed. at Melbourne Church of England Gram. schl., and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut. 2nd Scottish Horse, S. African War; inspr. of pol., E. Africa Prot., May, 1905; asst. dist. supt. of pol., 1st July, 1906; supt. of pol., 1910.
PILCHER, LIEUT.-COL. THOMAS DAVID.— Northumberland fusiliers, p.s.c., 1st comsn., 1879; capt., 1886; major, 1897; temp. lieut.-col., 1897; D.A.A.G., Dub. dist., 1895-7; W. African F.F., Oct., 1897.
